verdict.maxNextSpend: $35-$70 for product-form A/B, plus sample-only spend after clarity gate

sources.confidence: High for language; Low for product format
sources.name: Rucia PickFu Concept Pilot, 50 US women 18-34
sources.observed: A 26 / C 24. Open-ended comments support fragrance clarity, room mood language, quiz/match potential, and visual attention. The test did not isolate reed vs aroma-stone product form.
sources.decision: Use as concept-language evidence only; do not use as product-form proof.

sources.name: Google Trends US 12-month pull via pytrends
sources.observed: Successful pull on 2026-06-15: essential oil diffuser 26.51, reed diffuser 23.04, crystal diffuser 1.94, aroma stone diffuser 0.11, lava stone diffuser 0.06 average relative interest. A later expanded pull hit Google 429 and was not used.
sources.decision: Search demand strongly favors known diffuser language. Aroma stone is too small to use as the main acquisition keyword.

sources.name: Grand View Research, Home Fragrance Market
sources.observed: Home fragrance is a large and growing market; North America is identified as a leading region. Demand drivers include personalized living spaces, relaxation, home decor, and online retail.
sources.decision: Supports entering home fragrance broadly, not a specific proof for crystal aroma stones.

sources.name: ASINSIGHT, Reed Diffusers for Home Amazon sample
sources.observed: The sample shows heavy product density and price concentration, especially $10-$20 and $20-$50 tiers.
sources.decision: Reed has demand proof and competition proof. It is easier to explain but more exposed to price comparison.

sources.name: The Spruce, Best Reed Diffusers Tested
sources.observed: Test criteria center on scent strength, space size, longevity, vessel design, oil ingredients, and refill availability. Examples span budget, design, large-room, office, bathroom, and seasonal uses.
sources.decision: Reed category has mature consumer evaluation criteria. Any premium product must win on performance, scent, design, and refill logic.

sources.name: Homes & Gardens, Best Reed Diffusers
sources.observed: Current guide compares tested reed diffusers from roughly $32.99 to $250, with strength ratings and use cases from affordable to luxury.
sources.decision: Reed can support premium prices, but premium is earned through scent quality, brand trust, vessel, longevity, and editor/social proof.

sources.name: MUJI USA, Aroma Stone
sources.observed: Aroma Stone priced at $12.90. Review summary praises minimal design and pleasant scent for a few days; some users say scent fades quickly.
sources.decision: Aroma stone is understood as small, simple, close-space scenting. Scent duration is a known weakness.

sources.name: Dorai Home, Scent Stone
sources.observed: Dorai positions Scent Stone as a minimal no-cord way to bring a soft scent into a small space. Essential oil is not included.
sources.decision: Stone format has modern design legitimacy, but the strongest use case is soft scent in small spaces.

sources.name: At-Aroma USA, Stone mini travel set
sources.observed: Usage instruction: put 3-5 drops of essential oil directly on the stone. Small number of reviews, 4.5 based on two reviews.
sources.decision: Stone format is credible as a travel / small personal scent object, not yet proven as a room-filling hero.

sources.name: Etsy, Aroma Stone Diffuser Clean Fragrance Gift Set
sources.observed: $15 listing, Star Seller, 5-star listing signal. Materials include fragrance oil, essential oil, gift box, metal tin, and scented salt crystals. Copy frames it as giftable home fragrance and wellness gift.
sources.decision: Stone/crystal fragrance sets can sell as gifts, but Etsy evidence is niche marketplace proof, not mass-market proof.

sources.name: Amazon, Runyangshi crystal aroma stone diffuser lamp
sources.observed: $29.99, 3.9 stars from 13 ratings. Product combines crystal night light, gift packaging, and crystal/energy claims.
sources.decision: There is public marketplace supply for crystal aroma objects, but ratings/review depth are thin and claims style is riskier than Rucia should use.

sources.name: Amazon, Himalayan Salt Lamp with Aromatherapy Tray
sources.observed: Positive reviews mention beauty, ambiance, sturdy lamp, and aromatherapy tray. Negative/critical evidence includes scent not being noticeable and damage/cracking in shipping.
sources.decision: Crystal/stone objects create visual value and shipping/performance risks at the same time.

sources.name: Walmart, aroma stone how-to-use assortment
sources.observed: Visible aroma stone / crystal diffuser options appear at low prices such as from $5.91, $10.99, $11.39, and $17.99.
sources.decision: Low-price marketplace supply exists. Rucia needs strong design, scent, and gift system to avoid looking like a commodity crystal accessory.

sources.name: Scent Land, Custom Aroma Stone Diffuser Supplier Guide
sources.observed: Supplier guide frames aroma stones as passive diffusers for small spaces, travel, cars, wardrobes, gifts, hotel amenities, and promotional sets; typical MOQ examples include 300-1,000 pcs depending on gypsum/ceramic and customization.
sources.decision: Supply feasibility exists, but MOQ/material/scent performance must be quoted before any commitment.

sources.name: Pew Research Center, Spirituality Among Americans
sources.observed: 26% of U.S. adults believe spiritual energy can reside in objects such as crystals, jewels, or stones.
sources.decision: Crystal language has cultural familiarity, but it should stay light and non-medical.

sources.name: Religion News Service / Springtide crystal signal
sources.observed: RNS reports Springtide data that 44% of young people ages 13-25 engage with crystals or herbs as a spiritual exercise; 21% at least weekly.
sources.decision: Supports light crystal/spiritual interest among young people, not proof of purchase intent for a crystal aroma kit.

sources.name: McKinsey, Future of Wellness Trends 2025
sources.observed: McKinsey frames wellness as a daily, personalized practice for Gen Z and millennials and emphasizes value, quality, efficacy, and social proof.
sources.decision: Young buyers may try wellness-adjacent products, but Rucia must show quality and credible sensory performance.

sources.name: WSL Strategic Retail, Gen Z Fragrance Boom
sources.observed: Reports Circana-linked signals: 73% of Gen Z wears fragrance at least three times per week; TikTok and Instagram are major fragrance purchase influences.
sources.decision: Supports fragrance and social discovery for the target audience, but this is personal fragrance evidence more than home fragrance evidence.

sources.name: NEST New York, Reed Diffusers and Refills
sources.observed: NEST sells premium reed diffusers and diffuser refills, using scent, vessel, longevity and refill logic to justify a premium home-fragrance position.
sources.decision: Supports a scent-led product system where the same fragrance can create starter, performance and refill paths.

sources.name: P.F. Candle Co., Fresh Collection and Ingredients
sources.observed: P.F. Candle Co. communicates ingredient standards for home fragrance and sells multiple fragrance formats across a recognizable scent world.
sources.decision: Supports transparent fragrance standards and multi-format scent worlds as trust builders.

sources.name: Voluspa, Shop by Scent / Diffusers / Candles
sources.observed: Voluspa organizes home fragrance around scent families and sells formats including candles, oil diffusers and personal fragrance.
sources.decision: Shows that mature fragrance brands often use scent as the organizing unit, with physical formats serving different usage occasions.

sources.name: IFRA Standards
sources.observed: IFRA describes its standards as a global risk-management system for fragrance ingredients, including restrictions, limits and bans. Companies remain responsible for safe use.
sources.decision: Any supplier shortlist must provide IFRA documentation or equivalent fragrance compliance support before sample approval.

sources.name: Registrar Corp, FDA Regulations for Aromatherapy
sources.observed: Aromatherapy products can trigger drug-like regulatory scrutiny when claims imply treatment of pain, anxiety, insomnia, nausea or disease conditions.
sources.decision: Rucia should use room mood / vibe / ritual language and avoid medical or therapeutic outcome claims.

sources.name: FTC Policy Statement Regarding Advertising Substantiation
sources.observed: FTC policy requires advertisers to have a reasonable basis for objective claims before making them.
sources.decision: Claims such as room coverage, longevity, non-toxic, safe, pet-safe or mood outcomes need evidence or should be softened.

sources.name: VCA Hospitals, Essential Oil and Liquid Potpourri Poisoning in Cats
sources.observed: VCA warns that certain essential oils and liquid potpourri can be poisonous to cats through ingestion, skin exposure or other contact.
sources.decision: Do not claim pet-safe. Add keep-away warnings and avoid marketing the product for pet households without clear evidence.

sources.name: ASPCA, Essentials of Essential Oils Around Pets
sources.observed: ASPCA warns that concentrated essential oils can be dangerous for pets, depending on oil, exposure and animal.
sources.decision: Fragrance-oil and essential-oil safety language must include responsible placement, ventilation and keep-out-of-reach guidance.

sources.name: CPSC, Small Parts and Choking Hazard Labeling FAQs
sources.observed: CPSC small-parts rules address choking, aspiration and ingestion hazards for children under three.
sources.decision: Small crystals, chips and removable stones require clear age and choking warnings, and the product should not be positioned for children.

sources.name: International Gem Society, Gemstone Toxicity Table
sources.observed: IGS publishes a toxicity table covering hundreds of gems and describes hazards related to handling, cutting, ingestion and exposure.
sources.decision: Use conservative stone selection, avoid risky raw minerals and never instruct customers to put stones in drinking water or on skin.

sources.name: National Geographic, Ethical Crystal Buying
sources.observed: National Geographic reports concerns around crystal mining, worker exploitation, armed conflict and traceability gaps.
sources.decision: Rucia needs a sourcing note, supplier declarations and plain stone disclosure instead of vague rare/healing-stone mystique.

sources.name: GIA Gem Encyclopedia
sources.observed: GIA provides public gem education and identification references.
sources.decision: Use mainstream gem names and education language for credibility; avoid exaggerated crystal-power claims.

sources.name: Rucia 1688 screenshot scout, 2026-06-15
sources.observed: User-provided 1688 screenshots showed complete-looking crystal aroma gift sets roughly ¥18.5-74.5, realistic benchmark range ¥27-58, and many low-price component/bait listings below ¥10.
sources.decision: Supply exists. The next supplier step is itemized RFQ and samples, not MOQ commitment.

evidenceRatings: Home fragrance and fragrance usage are real; this does not pick the winning product format.
Reed diffuser has much stronger known search/category language.
Amazon and editorial guides show mature competition from budget to luxury.
Pew/Springtide support cultural familiarity, not direct purchase intent.
MUJI, Dorai, Etsy, Amazon, Walmart show supply and some buyer use, but small search demand and limited review depth.
Evidence repeatedly points to subtle, small-space scenting and fade risk.
Mature fragrance brands organize around scent and sell multiple formats; Rucia still needs a small concept test before launching multiple SKUs.
Aroma stone has low search demand, so visual education must do the work that search keywords cannot do.
Crystal interest exists, but origin, toxicity, small parts, pets and claims need strict guardrails.
Valid for fragrance + room mood + quiz language; invalid for product-form decision.
Reed has premium comparables; crystal stone needs stronger perceived value test.
China and OEM evidence say it can be made; docs, MOQ, leakage, breakage and scent oil quality remain unknown.
hypotheses: H1
北美目标用户会买 home fragrance / room scent 来改变空间状态。
Supported
大盘、媒体测试、PickFu room mood comments 均支持。
可以继续用 room reset / room mood 作为卖点。
H2
轻玄学、晶石、scent sign 能增加记忆点和礼物感。
Partially supported
Pew/Springtide 支持文化土壤；PickFu 只弱支持 quiz/ritual，不支持晶石为主驱动。
晶石做第二层，不做主功效。
H3
晶石扩香石比藤条有更大市场潜力。
Not proven
搜索需求和成熟购买语言目前更支持藤条。扩香石只有差异化潜力证据。
必须做产品形态 A/B 测试。
H4
扩香石能满足 room reset 的香味表现。
Not proven / high risk
公开证据多次指向 subtle / small-space / fades quickly。
必须做 scent throw 样品测试。
H5
用户愿意为晶石扩香石礼盒付 $49-$69。
Not proven
Etsy/MUJI/Dorai/Amazon stone benchmark多在 $13-$30；高价需要礼盒系统和香味证明。
必须做价格敏感度和 value perception 测试。
H6
供应链可做小批量高质感礼盒。
Partially supported
1688 和 OEM guide 支持可做；合规文件、漏液、破损、MOQ 未验证。
先 RFQ + 样品，不签 MOQ。
H7
同一香味下同时测试扩香石、藤条和全套礼盒，能降低方向选错风险。
Plausible / not proven
NEST、P.F. Candle、Voluspa 等成熟品牌支持 scent-led multi-format 逻辑；Rucia 的用户偏好还没测。
下一轮概念测试用同一香味跑三形态对照。
H8
强视觉教育能把 Rucia 和 crystal aroma stone 这个品类绑定。
Plausible / not proven
低搜索词说明用户不会主动找；PickFu 支持视觉冲击力和概念清晰度的重要性。
测试图必须包含产品结构、3 步用法、房间场景和 scent strength 预期。
H9
晶石安全和产地问题可以被透明规则解决。
Partially supported
IGS、CPSC、ASPCA/VCA、NatGeo 都说明有可管理风险，但需要材料选择、警示和供应商文件。
建立 Stone Safety & Sourcing Note，供应链 RFQ 必须要文件。

validationGates.name: Gate 1: 产品形态三选测试
validationGates.method: 同一套 VI、同一 hero scent、同一画面质量，A=Crystal Aroma Stone Kit，B=Reed Room Diffuser，C=Full Room Mood Set。50-100 位美国女性 18-34。只问更想点、看得懂、相信能香、是否适合送人、是否值 $49-$79。
validationGates.pass: Stone 或 Full Set 必须赢在 click / unique / gift / visual memory；Reed 若赢在 scent belief，就把 Reed 作为性能形态保留。
validationGates.stop: Stone 被大量说成 crystal decor、doesn't look like fragrance、won't scent a room，暂停把 Stone 设为 hero。

validationGates.name: Gate 2: 真实样品 scent throw
validationGates.budget: 先 2-3 家供应商样品 + 国际快递；暂不 MOQ
validationGates.method: 测试扩香石、陶瓷/石膏容器、香氛油在床头、浴室、小卧室、客厅的 1h / 24h / 7d 表现。记录闻不到、刚好、太冲、是否残留油渍。
validationGates.pass: 至少在小卧室/床头/浴室能稳定表达 scent presence；如果只能近距离闻到，产品定位降级为 desk/nightstand ritual。
validationGates.stop: 香味 24h 内消失、油渍明显、石材破损、用户需要频繁补油且觉得麻烦。

validationGates.name: Gate 3: $49-$69 value test
validationGates.method: 给用户看礼盒结构、香氛油容量、补充装价格和包装，做 Van Westendorp / Gabor-Granger 小样本测试。
validationGates.pass: $49 可被接受，$59 有部分可接受，$69 需要更强礼盒/赠礼理由。
validationGates.stop: 用户将其锚定到 $15-$30 的 MUJI/Etsy/Amazon stone accessory。

validationGates.name: Gate 4: 供应商文件和报价
validationGates.method: 向 6-8 家供应商要分项报价：容器、扩香石、晶石、香氛油、滴管瓶、卡片、内托、外盒、贴标、SDS/MSDS、IFRA、flash point、破损/漏液包装方案。
validationGates.pass: 至少 2 家能给英文文件、合理 MOQ、分项成本和样品周期。
validationGates.stop: 只能做国内礼品、没有文件、只会报总价、无法解释香氛油基底和运输。

formatArchitectureRows: Crystal Aroma Stone Kit
视觉、礼物、轻仪式、桌面/床头小空间
差异化强，内容好拍，适合绑定 scent sign。
弱香、用户不知道怎么用、容易被锚定到 $15-$30 小摆件。
下一轮必须作为 A 方案测试，但文案先写 small-space / personal room ritual。
Reed Room Diffuser
稳定扩香、熟悉品类、降低理解成本
用户知道怎么用，也更相信能让房间香。
同质化强，Amazon/Target/成熟香氛品牌价格锚点明显。
下一轮作为 B 方案测试；若 scent belief 明显胜出，保留为性能形态。
Full Room Mood Set
高客单礼盒：Stone + Reed + refill/card
同时解决视觉记忆和扩香表现，有更强 gift / bundle 感。
SKU、成本、包装、破损和教育复杂度上升。
下一轮作为 C 方案测试；若胜出，首发可做 limited gift set，但先不大货。
Scent Oil Refill
复购和毛利
复购更可能来自香味，不来自晶石本体。
如果首购体验香味弱，refill 没意义。
等 scent liking 和 scent throw 过关后再验证。
Mini Discovery / Scent Card
低价试香和内容采样
降低试错成本，能为 creator/gift guide 提供入口。
如果太像香水小样，会削弱礼盒溢价。
暂作为后置工具，不进入首发 SKU。
educationRows: 一张图看到：香氛油瓶、扩香石/容器、晶石、结果卡、礼盒。
让用户 3 秒内优先识别为 home fragrance，避免看成水晶摆件。
Take quiz -> pick scent sign -> add drops / place diffuser。
降低教育成本，避免用户问“这个怎么香”。
明确 small space / desk / nightstand / bathroom / bedroom；客厅 coverage 需要测试通过再说。
提前管理 scent strength 预期，避免差评来自过度承诺。
Soft / Medium / Room-Filling 三档图标；Stone 默认 soft-medium，Reed 默认 medium-room。
把弱香风险提前变成透明选择和购买预期。
用图标讲清：flameless、scent oil included、crystal card、refillable、room mood。
让 TikTok 缩略图和货架图也能被理解。
15 秒视频：打开盒子、滴油、放置、刷新香味、注意事项。
用视觉教育绑定品类，不靠长文解释。
riskSolutionRows: 香味太弱
扩香石公开证据多指向 soft / small-space / fades quickly。
不要承诺全屋扩香；测试同香三形态；Stone 负责仪式和小空间，Reed 负责稳定 room scent；样品测试记录 1h/24h/7d。
Stone 只能近闻，且用户觉得麻烦。
市场教育成本
aroma stone diffuser 搜索需求很低，用户不会主动找。
用 home fragrance / room scent / flameless diffuser 作为入口词；把 crystal aroma stone 做品牌化命名；页面和包装使用 3 步视觉说明。
用户仍说不清是什么，或看成 decor。
低价锚点
MUJI、Etsy、Walmart、Amazon stone 对用户有 $13-$30 锚点。
首发卖 kit / gift / quiz / scent system，不卖单个 stone；价值必须包含香氛油、卡片、礼盒、refill path。
用户将 $49-$69 直接和普通小香薰/摆件比较。
晶石产地和伦理
公开报道提示 crystal sourcing 有劳动、冲突和追溯风险。
只使用可解释的常见石材；要求供应商 stone declaration；公开 Stone Safety & Sourcing Note；避免 rare/healing 叙事。
供应商无法说明石材来源或把仿石当天然石。
晶石材质安全
某些矿物可能有毒、易碎、水溶或不适合接触油/水。
首发只用保守清单：clear quartz / rose quartz / amethyst / aventurine 等抛光碎石；避免 malachite、galena、cinnabar、raw pyrite 等高风险矿物；不做 crystal water。
样品掉色、掉粉、异味、尖锐碎片或材料不明。
宠物/儿童风险
精油/香氛油对宠物和小孩可能有误食、接触和呼吸风险。
不做 pet-safe claim；包装写 keep out of reach；小件加 choking warning；油瓶防漏；页面建议通风和避免宠物接触。
用户大量询问 pet safe，而供应商无法提供安全文件。
香氛合规与运输
香氛油涉及 IFRA/SDS/MSDS、flash point、过敏原和运输信息。
RFQ 必要字段：SDS/MSDS、IFRA certificate、allergen list、flash point、oil base、leakage test、export packaging。
供应商只会报总价，不能提供英文文件。
功效/玄学合规
治疗、焦虑、睡眠、净化、保护、招财等客观或医疗化承诺需要证据且风险高。
语言使用 vibe、room mood、scent sign、ritual、reset；避免 heal/cure/anxiety/sleep treatment/protection/guaranteed luck。
创作者或落地页把产品讲成治疗/转运工具。
